message:
refactor(abi): route internal libc calls through interposed ABI functions

Replace direct libc::memcpy, libc::memmove, libc::memset, libc::strlen,
libc::strcmp, libc::malloc, and libc::free calls with their frankenlibc
interposed equivalents (crate::string_abi::*, crate::malloc_abi::*)
across fortify_abi, glibc_internal_abi, inet_abi, rpc_abi, search_abi,
stdio_abi, stdlib_abi, string_abi, unistd_abi, and wchar_abi modules.

This ensures that internal library operations pass through the membrane
validation pipeline (when runtime is active) rather than bypassing it
via raw libc calls, giving consistent observability and safety
guarantees for all memory and string operations regardless of whether
they originate from user code or internal glibc-compatible helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
